text: The surgeon performs a medial to lateral dissection using a no-touch technique along the tongue fascial plane, preserving the retroperitoneal fascia. The right ureter and gonadal vessels are identified. The ileocolic vessels are ligated at the mesenteric root. The third portion of the duodenum and the head of the pancreas are visualized and separated from the mesentery.

The surgeon delicately dissects the truncal alley, ensuring the anterior superior pancreatic duodenal vein is tight to prevent bleeding during the mesocolon traction maneuver. The right colic muscles are identified and ligated, and the right gas colic is opened to recover the D-plane.

The surgeon exposes the retroperitoneal plane caudally and dissects the last jejunal loop. The right paracolic gutter is liberated, and the transverse colon is dissected. The specimen is extracted through the phanacin incision. The ileocolic stump is pulled upwards using a needle, ensuring the posterior wall of the anastomosis is secure before stapling. The stapler is fired, and the anastomosis is checked internally.
The surgeon closes the anatomy with two planes of continuous suture, completing the closure of the surgical site.
